title: Egift card email was in my inbox and now it's gone
creator: sandy0831
content:
I received an email on 02/25/2026 that was a egift card from a local small business. The egift card was from with Square account. When I went back into my inbox the next day to use the egift card, the email was gone. I looked in all my folders and didn't see it anywhere. How can an email just disappear? When I contacted the small business, they said they can't honor the egift card without the 16 digit # on the egift card. I showed them the receipt for the purchase but it doesn't not show the 16 digit code for the ecard, only shows the purchase of a gift card. So how do I find this email with the egift card?

this was the email address from the purchase invoice. noreply@messaging.squareup.com

I don't recall the actual email address the egift card was from other than I saw it was from a Square account.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/145.0.0.0 Safari/537.36;os:Windows 11

creator: dc.hart
content:
Thunderbird 147.0 (64-bit) OS: Linux; Fedora 43 Settings:

  • smtp.gmail.com
  • port 465
  • SSL/TLS
  • OAuth2
  • full email address

Something is presumably corrupted. I have had this account for many, many years, so I have accumulated many filters and other data.

title: There is no setting in the calendar for a recurring date on the "last workday" of each month
creator: stuagux
content:
There is currently no setting in the Thunderbird calendar for a recurring date on the "last workday" of each month. This is very necessary for certain recurring dates, such as payments issued online monthly on that date.

This function is included e.g. in the Nextcloud calendar app, but not in the Thunderbird mail calendar.

Is this something coming soon?

Thank you very much.

title: How can I add a second outlook email account in Thunderbird?
creator: naomisharpns
content: I have an outlook work email account in Thunderbird. Now I need to add another for a charity role. The first/work one is fine, but I can only receive emails on the second/charity account. I've tried different setting for the outgoing server but I don't really know what I'm doing with that - can you help please? Thank you.

title: Cannot import data to Thunderbird address book
creator: doc.lock81
content: Just started using Thunderbird, coming from MS Outlook. I have exported my contact details in Outlook to a .csv file, and tried to use this as the import source to transfer the details to Thunderbird address book. I have checked the fields to transfer, but none end up being passed over. The .csv file appears to hold the correct data with the correct field headers. Although I do not have many contacts, transferring them individually by hand seems a very old-fashioned thing to have to do lol
